content.problemStatement:
Alice likes to shop online. The main reason is that she gets a lot of discounts! There are **M** online shops from where she purchases her items. The prices of the same item vary in both the shops. She needs to buy **N** items in all. And each she can buy each item in any one of the shops. All the shops offer certain discounts. If she buys the ith item in one shop, she gets a discount coupon for the i+1th item in the same shop. But if she purchases the i+1th item from a different shop, she cannot use the discount coupon.
 For example, if she buys the ith item in Shop j and gets a discount of **X** rupees. Let the price of i+1th item be **Y** rupees in shop j and **Z** rupees in shop k. If she purchases the i+1th item from shop j she can use the discount coupon and hence, will have to pay **Y-X** rupees. If she purchases the i+1th item from shop k she cannot use the discount coupon and hence, will have to pay **Z** rupees.
**Note:** If the discount given is more than price of the item in the shop, i.e. if **Y-X** < 0, the item is given free but she does NOT get any money back.

Alice wants to spend as less as possible and wants your help. She starts buying from item 1 and goes on till item N in that order. Your task is simple. Tell her the least amount to spend to get all the **N** items.

Input:**T**
**N****M****N****M**jthithithjth
**N****M**jthithithjth

Output:**N**
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with two integers N and M, where N is the number of items and M is the number of shops. Then, for each item, there are M lines, each containing the price and discount of the item in a shop.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the minimum amount Alice needs to spend to buy all N items.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, Alice can buy the first item from shop 1 for 3 rupees and the second item from shop 1 for 0 rupees (using the discount coupon). For the second test case, Alice can buy the first item from shop 1 for 1 rupee and the second item from shop 1 for 1 rupee.
For this test case, Alice can buy the first item from shop 1 for 10 rupees, the second item from shop 2 for 15 rupees, and the third item from shop 3 for 40 rupees.
For this test case, Alice can buy the first item from shop 1 for 100 rupees and the second item from shop 1 for 100 rupees (using the discount coupon).

solution.approach: This problem can be solved using dynamic programming. The idea is to maintain a 2D array dp where dp[i][j] represents the minimum amount Alice needs to spend to buy the first i items with the last item bought from shop j. We iterate over each item and for each item, we iterate over each shop. For each shop, we calculate the minimum amount Alice needs to spend to buy the current item from that shop by considering two cases: either she buys the current item from the same shop as the previous item or from a different shop. We update dp[i][j] with the minimum of these two cases.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N * M) per test case, O(T * N * M) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N * M) — the dominant structure is the dp array.
